forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+project: ai-panel
+title: WebSocket transport for the ACP agent server
+---
+## What
+The AI panel's ACP client runs entirely in the webview (TypeScript). A webview cannot spawn a subprocess or use stdio, so it cannot use ACP's standard stdio transport. The Rust ACP agent server must expose its JSON-RPC message stream over a transport a browser connects to natively: a **WebSocket**. Tauri is not involved in the ACP data path at all.
+
+- `sah agent acp` (`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/agent/acp.rs`) currently calls `AcpServer::start_with_streams(stdin(), stdout())`. Add a WebSocket listen mode â€” e.g. `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0`: bind a loopback TCP listener, accept a WebSocket upgrade, and run the ACP agent with the WebSocket carrying newline-delimited JSON-RPC (the same ndjson framing as stdio per ACP's transports spec â€” messages delimited by `\n`, no embedded newlines).
+- Use `tokio-tungstenite` (workspace dep). Adapt the WebSocket to the stream shape `AcpServer` needs, or add `start_with_websocket`.
+- Report the bound port (stdout line and/or exit-on-failure). Loopback-only bind.
+- stdio mode stays the default and is unchanged.
+
+This is the "server in Rust exposes the stream" half of the architecture. Reference: ACP transports â€” https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/transports
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0` binds a loopback port, accepts a WebSocket connection, and serves ACP JSON-RPC over it.
+- [ ] ndjson framing over the WebSocket matches the stdio transport (newline-delimited, no embedded newlines).
+- [ ] The bound port is discoverable by the launcher (printed or otherwise surfaced).
+- [ ] stdio mode is unchanged and remains the default; `cargo build` clean.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test: start `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0`, connect a WebSocket client, send `initialize`, assert a valid ACP `initialize` response with negotiated protocol version.
+- [ ] Test that stdio mode (`start_with_streams`) still works unchanged.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli` (and the affected agent crate) is green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the WebSocket `initialize` round-trip test first.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,28 +4,27 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '80'
 project: ai-panel
-title: WebSocket transport for the ACP agent server
+title: In-process ACP agent served over a localhost WebSocket
 ---
 ## What
-The AI panel's ACP client runs entirely in the webview (TypeScript). A webview cannot spawn a subprocess or use stdio, so it cannot use ACP's standard stdio transport. The Rust ACP agent server must expose its JSON-RPC message stream over a transport a browser connects to natively: a **WebSocket**. Tauri is not involved in the ACP data path at all.
-
-- `sah agent acp` (`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/agent/acp.rs`) currently calls `AcpServer::start_with_streams(stdin(), stdout())`. Add a WebSocket listen mode â€” e.g. `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0`: bind a loopback TCP listener, accept a WebSocket upgrade, and run the ACP agent with the WebSocket carrying newline-delimited JSON-RPC (the same ndjson framing as stdio per ACP's transports spec â€” messages delimited by `\n`, no embedded newlines).
-- Use `tokio-tungstenite` (workspace dep). Adapt the WebSocket to the stream shape `AcpServer` needs, or add `start_with_websocket`.
-- Report the bound port (stdout line and/or exit-on-failure). Loopback-only bind.
-- stdio mode stays the default and is unchanged.
+The ACP agent runs **inside the kanban-app process** â€” not as an external subprocess. The Tauri Rust backend builds the ACP agent in-process and exposes it on a loopback WebSocket; the webview's TypeScript ACP client connects to that WebSocket. Tauri IPC is NOT in the ACP data path â€” the data path is a plain WebSocket.

-This is the "server in Rust exposes the stream" half of the architecture. Reference: ACP transports â€” https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/transports
+- New `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_ws.rs`. Add to `apps/kanban-app/Cargo.toml`: `swissarmyhammer-agent`, `agent-client-protocol`, `tokio-tungstenite` (workspace deps).
+- Host a loopback WebSocket server (`tokio-tungstenite`, bind `127.0.0.1:0`); report the bound port.
+- On a WebSocket connection, build the ACP agent in-process via `swissarmyhammer_agent::create_agent(&model_config, None)` â€” `mcp_config` is `None`; the kanban MCP server is delivered over ACP in `newSession.mcpServers`, never baked in. `create_agent` dispatches Claude Code (claude-agent) vs local llama (llama-agent).
+- Run the ACP **agent side** over the WebSocket: adapt the WebSocket to the `AsyncRead`/`AsyncWrite` byte stream the agent server consumes, with newline-delimited JSON framing. The agent servers already expose a `start_with_streams(read, write)` stdio pattern (`llama_agent::acp::AcpServer`, claude-agent's server) â€” adapt that to the WS stream (e.g. via `ws_stream_tungstenite` or a small adapter).
+- Gate `llama-agent` behind a Cargo feature (`ai-local-models`, default off) so the standard build stays light â€” claude-agent is always available; local llama is opt-in.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0` binds a loopback port, accepts a WebSocket connection, and serves ACP JSON-RPC over it.
-- [ ] ndjson framing over the WebSocket matches the stdio transport (newline-delimited, no embedded newlines).
-- [ ] The bound port is discoverable by the launcher (printed or otherwise surfaced).
-- [ ] stdio mode is unchanged and remains the default; `cargo build` clean.
+- [ ] A loopback WebSocket server runs in the kanban-app process; a WebSocket client connecting to it completes the ACP `initialize` handshake against an in-process agent.
+- [ ] The agent is built via `swissarmyhammer_agent::create_agent`; Claude Code works without the `ai-local-models` feature.
+- [ ] No external agent subprocess â€” the agent runs in the kanban-app process (claude-agent spawning `claude` internally is fine).
+- [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean both with and without `--features ai-local-models`.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test: start `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0`, connect a WebSocket client, send `initialize`, assert a valid ACP `initialize` response with negotiated protocol version.
-- [ ] Test that stdio mode (`start_with_streams`) still works unchanged.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli` (and the affected agent crate) is green.
+- [ ] Integration test (`apps/kanban-app/tests/`): start the WS agent server, connect a WebSocket client, send `initialize`, assert a valid ACP `initialize` response with a negotiated protocol version.
+- [ ] Build/feature test: `kanban-app` builds with and without `ai-local-models`.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` â€” write the WebSocket `initialize` round-trip test first.
\ No newline at end of file

The agent servers already expose a `start_with_streams(read, write)` stdio pattern (`llama_agent::acp::AcpServer`, claude-agent's server) â€” adapt that to the WS stream (e.g. via `ws_stream_tungstenite` or a small adapter).

+- `sah agent acp` (`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/agent/acp.rs`) currently calls `AcpServer::start_with_streams(stdin(), stdout())`. +- Use `tokio-tungstenite` (workspace dep). Adapt the WebSocket to the stream shape `AcpServer` needs, or add `start_with_websocket`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A loopback WebSocket server runs in the kanban-app process; a WebSocket client connecting to it completes the ACP `initialize` handshake against an in-process agent.
-- [ ] The agent is built via `swissarmyhammer_agent::create_agent`; Claude Code works without the `ai-local-models` feature.
-- [ ] No external agent subprocess â€” the agent runs in the kanban-app process (claude-agent spawning `claude` internally is fine).
-- [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean both with and without `--features ai-local-models`.
+- [ ] `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0` binds a loopback port, accepts a WebSocket connection, and serves ACP JSON-RPC over it.
+- [ ] ndjson framing over the WebSocket matches the stdio transport (newline-delimited, no embedded newlines).
+- [ ] The bound port is discoverable by the launcher (printed or otherwise surfaced).
+- [ ] stdio mode is unchanged and remains the default; `cargo build` clean.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test (`apps/kanban-app/tests/`): start the WS agent server, connect a WebSocket client, send `initialize`, assert a valid ACP `initialize` response with a negotiated protocol version.
-- [ ] Build/feature test: `kanban-app` builds with and without `ai-local-models`.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.
+- [ ] Integration test: start `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0`, connect a WebSocket client, send `initialize`, assert a valid ACP `initialize` response with negotiated protocol version.
+- [ ] Test that stdio mode (`start_with_streams`) still works unchanged.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli` (and the affected agent crate) is green.

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` â€” write the WebSocket `initialize` round-trip test first.
